label: Harmony0
title: Harmony0
bodyHtml:
What Harmony does

Harmony lets a BepInEx mod attach code to methods that already exist in the game. You are not editing Assembly-CSharp.dll on disk. You are telling Harmony: when the game calls this method, also run my method before it, after it, or around it.

That is how CUCoreLib and most Casualties Unknown mods add behavior. You find the target method in dnSpyEx or the decompiled reference, then write a small patch class that points at that exact type and method name.

[HarmonyPatch(typeof(Body), "Eat")] // Look on the right pane for an example

Prefix patches

A Prefix runs before the original game method. Use it when you need to validate inputs, record state before vanilla changes it, tweak arguments, or optionally stop the original method from running.

If a prefix returns void, the original method keeps running. If it returns bool, returning false skips the original method. Skipping vanilla is powerful, but it is also where bugs breed, so only do it when you are intentionally replacing the method.

[HarmonyPrefix]
private static void Prefix(Body __instance)
{
    // Make the player happier every time before they eat, without skipping the normal Eat code.
    __instance.happiness += 1f;
}

Postfix patches

A Postfix runs after the original game method finishes. Use it when vanilla should do its normal work first and your mod only needs to react to the final state.

Postfixes are usually safer for additive behavior: logging, registering extra content after vanilla setup, refreshing UI after a list rebuild, or changing a result after the original method calculated it.

[HarmonyPostfix]
private static void Postfix(Body __instance)
{
    Plugin.Logger.LogInfo($"Hunger is now {__instance.hunger}");
}

References and special parameters

Harmony matches patch parameters by name. Normal parameter names match the original method's parameters. Special names give you access to extra pieces of the call.

Parameter What it means Common use
__instanceThe object the original method is running on.Read or change fields on a non-static class instance.
__resultThe value the original method returns.Read it in a postfix, or set it when replacing a method.
ref hungerAmountA writable reference to an original argument named hungerAmount.Change an argument before vanilla uses it.
__stateA value passed from your prefix to your postfix.Compare before and after values without storing global data.

The ref keyword matters. Without it, you receive a copy. With it, your patch can write back to the original argument or result.

[HarmonyPrefix]
private static void Prefix(Body __instance, ref float hungerAmount)
{
    // Make all foods half as filling (if >6 satiation), before running the normal Eat code.
    hungerAmount -= Mathf.Max(3f, hungerAmount * 0.5f);
}

Closing Notes

  • Make sure the decompiled game is up to date.
  • BepInEx's Chainloader can skip your prefixes if there are more then one patch for the same method, i.e., if multiple mods try to patch the same method.
  • Keep patch classes static unless you have a specific reason not to.
  • Prefer postfixes for additive behavior and prefixes for input changes or intentional overrides.
  • Try to avoid patching high-frequency methods like Update unless the work is tiny and carefully guarded.
